data.title: As a practitioner, implementation agents signal confidence and sprint routes double-failures to re-planning
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Implementation sub-agents emit a CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN:<reason> signal. When an agent emits UNCERTAIN twice on the same task within a sprint, sprint routes the task to implementation-plan for re-planning. Failure tracking is per story to prevent counter resets on task replacement.
**Why**: Today, sub-agents either guess (producing wrong code) or fail silently. An explicit confidence signal lets the system distinguish between transient failures (retry) and plan-level gaps (re-plan). Per-story tracking prevents infinite retry when implementation-plan creates replacement tasks with new IDs.
**Scope**:
- IN: CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN signal format in sub-agent output, sprint parsing of confidence signal, double-failure detection (per story, not per task ID), routing to implementation-plan, REPLAN_TRIGGER: failure comment
- OUT: Modifications to implementation-plan itself (Epic A), brainstorm escalation logic

## Done Definitions
- When this story is complete, implementation sub-agents emit CONFIDENT or UNCERTAIN:<reason> as a structured signal in their output
  ← Satisfies: "Implementation sub-agents emit a structured confidence signal"
- When this story is complete, sprint detects two UNCERTAIN signals on the same task within a sprint invocation and routes the task to implementation-plan for re-planning, tracked per story
  ← Satisfies: "When an agent emits UNCERTAIN twice... sprint routes the task to implementation-plan"
- When this story is complete, a REPLAN_TRIGGER: failure comment is recorded on the epic
  ← Satisfies: "each trigger records a structured REPLAN_TRIGGER"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations
- [Reliability] CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN is a new sub-agent output contract — all implementation agents must adopt it
- [Testing] Needs tests for: single UNCERTAIN (retry), double UNCERTAIN (re-plan), counter persistence across task replacement

## Escalation Policy
**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent. "High confidence" means clear evidence from the codebase or ticket context — not inference or reasonable assumption. When in doubt, stop and ask rather than guess.
